rationale: Acting on either leaves essentially the same PR: both correctly flag the undocumented `change` + includeRight_apply redundancy (real but trivial), and the simpa/WithConv hacks (fragile but documented, standard for this boilerplate); neither other finding in Review 1 is a real proof-quality gain—ext+induction+simp and the simp chains are idiomatic Mathlib patterns a maintainer would not demand refactored, so the extra explicit lemmas/rewrites would only add noise without benefit.
